Import TensorFlow and necessary augmentation functions including RandAugment from official/vision/ops/augment.py
Set a fixed random seed (e.g., 42) for reproducibility
Define input image batch size as 4 with height and width of 224 and 3 channels
Create a dummy input tensor of shape (4, 224, 224, 3) filled with constant or random values
Initialize RandAugment with a specific magnitude level (e.g., level=5) and a non-zero magnitude standard deviation (e.g., level_std=0.5)
Apply RandAugment augmentation to the input tensor
Extract or instrument the augmentation code to capture the internally computed magnitude values after the operation that adds the standard deviation noise

Specifically observe the line around https://github.com/tensorflow/models/blob/9fb4a4fbcd888616586ceffa1b8806ffe1126f54/official/vision/ops/augment.py#L1566 to verify if the magnitude standard deviation is properly scaled by level_std or just added as a raw random noise with std=1
Run multiple passes of augmentation to collect a distribution of applied magnitudes
Compute and assert that the standard deviation of the applied magnitude values is either exactly 0 or exactly 1, indicating the missing multiplication by level_std in the code
Log or print the observed standard deviation of magnitudes applied to confirm the bug reproducibility
Optionally, compare to a corrected implementation where the standard deviation is multiplied by level_std to show the difference in magnitude variability
Conclude that the magnitude standard deviation is not scaled properly and reproduces the bug as reported
